t_ch: 你们将岁阳及捉鬼小队的事告诉了晴霓。
t_en: You and the others tell Qingni about the heliobi and the ghost-hunting squad.

t_ch: 妈妈被岁阳附身了？我、我该怎么办？
t_en: Mom is being possessed by a heliobus? Th—Then what should I do?

t_ch: …晴霓小姐别紧张，我、我是十王司的判官，我会帮忙处理的。不过在此之前，你还听说了怎样的传言？
t_en: ...Stay calm, Miss Qingni. I—I am a Judge of the Ten-Lords Commission. I will handle this for you. But before that, what other rumors have you heard?

t_ch: 我听说妈妈从星槎上离开时，幽灵星槎上还有一位狐人飞行士，穿着天舶司的衣服。
t_en: I heard people saying that when Mom left the starskiff, there was a foxian pilot on the skiff as well. They were wearing the Sky-Faring Commission uniform.

t_ch: 岁阳不止附身在驭空大人身上。
t_en: Madam Yukong isn't the only one who is possessed.

t_ch: 有、有道理。
t_en: Y—You're right.

t_ch: 到底是谁啊，看得这么清楚？
t_en: Who is that rumormonger? They have pretty good eyesight.

t_ch: 是岩明大叔，他说自己那天在港口接待事务时偶然看到这事。
t_en: It's Uncle Yanming. He said he was receiving some guests at the port that day and incidentally saw it.

t_ch: 驭空大人平时都在司辰宫里处理公务，摇动同心火铃的话…那地方不妥吧？
t_en: Madam Yukong is often handling official business at the Palace of Astrum. If we ring the Harmonic Chime... that place might be a bit inconvenient, no?

t_ch: 当然啦，司辰宫是接待仙舟宾客的门面，几位就算除魔，也请不要在那儿闹出太大动静啊。
t_en: Most certainly. The Palace of Astrum is the official location where we receive guests from other Xianzhou ships. I understand you need to exorcise the demons, but please do not make a ruckus there.

t_ch: 也许我们可以把驭空大人单独约出来？
t_en: How about we ask Madam Yukong to come out alone?

t_ch: 好主意，走，现在就去司辰宫。
t_en: That's a good idea. Come on. Let's go to the Palace of Astrum now.

t_ch: 对了，一会你们去找妈妈的时候，我不能在场，不然她就知道我去找过幽灵星槎了。
t_en: Right, I cannot follow you to meet my mother. Otherwise, she will know that I went to investigate the phantom starskiff.

t_ch: 别怕，现在是她比较理亏…
t_en: Don't be scared. She's the one who broke her own rules...

t_ch: 这不是理亏不理亏的问题，你们千万别提我啊！
t_en: It doesn't matter what she did. Please don't mention me to her!

t_ch: 放心，我们是不会出卖你的…
t_en: Don't worry. We will not betray you...

t_ch: 我相信你们，这件事就拜托啦。
t_en: I trust you. I'm counting on you.

t_ch: 晴霓小姐放心，我身为十王司的判官，会妥善处理相关一切的。
t_en: Miss Qingni, don't worry. As a Judge of the Ten-Lords Commission, I promise to handle this case properly.

t_ch: 我在这里等各位消息。
t_en: I'll be waiting here for your good news.

t_ch: 我就在这里等着各位的消息。
t_en: Then I'll be waiting here for your good news.

t_ch: 我看你们出现在司辰宫，应该是来找我的。
t_en: Seeing you at the Palace of Astrum, I suppose you are here for me.

t_ch: 驭空大人，有件事想和你聊聊…
t_en: Madam Yukong, there's something we wish to speak with you...

t_ch: 是什么大事，竟然这么多人来找我？
t_en: What's the matter? You've brought quite a big group here.

t_ch: （直接摇动同心火铃）
t_en: (Rings the Harmonic Chime straightaway)

t_ch: 同心火铃响动起来，驭空转头看向了你，眼中闪过一丝疑惑。
t_en: The Harmonic Chime rings, and Yukong turns her head to you, a hint of doubt flashing across her eyes.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你在干什么啊？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, what are you doing?

t_ch: 打招呼的方式挺新颖啊。
t_en: That's an innovative way of saying hi.

t_ch: 驭空大人，我们在迴星港看到了幽灵星槎，您…您有什么头绪吗？
t_en: Madam Yukong, we saw a phantom starskiff at Stargazer Navalia. Do... Do you have any idea about it?

t_ch: （你这家伙问得也太直接了吧！）
t_en: (Could you be any less indiscreet!?)

t_ch: （我紧张！）
t_en: (I got nervous!)

t_ch: 抱歉，我对此并无头绪，想来这不过是荒诞的传说罢了。
t_en: I'm sorry. I have no idea about it. I believe it's just an absurd legend.

t_ch: 这事真真的！不过等我们走近瞧的时候，星槎消失了。
t_en: The skiff is r—r—real! But it disappeared the moment we got close to it.

t_ch: 也许只是光的幻觉，也许是一艘超速的星槎…诸位不如坦诚一点吧，为什么来找我？天舶司不登记人们虚构出来的星槎。
t_en: Perhaps it was just an illusion of light or a speedy starskiff... Please, why don't you be honest with me? What's the intention behind your visit? The Sky-Faring Commission does not register any imaginary starskiff.

t_ch: 有人曾目击你登上幽灵星槎。
t_en: Someone saw you board the phantom starskiff.

t_ch: 与我身形肖似的狐人不少，想来是看错了。
t_en: There are many foxians who have a similar figure as mine. I suppose that person is mistaken.

t_ch: 你还和船上的人有过交谈。
t_en: You had even talked to the people on the starskiff.

t_ch: 编的倒是有鼻子有眼。
t_en: Your story is pretty convincing.

t_ch: 认错驭空大人？不会吧？
t_en: Mistaken Madam Yukong for someone else? Are you serious?

t_ch: 街谈巷议就是这样，明明没有的事情，却说的十分确凿。
t_en: That's how rumors and street talks come about. Some things didn't happen, yet they can make it sound conclusive.

t_ch: 听说你不许职员谈论它…
t_en: I heard that you don't allow your staff to discuss the skiff...

t_ch: 迴星港里出没幽灵星槎，这样的怪谈毫不新鲜。打从我进天舶司，每十来年就会听说类似的传闻，花样各不相同，却都细节充分有鼻子有眼的。
t_en: A phantom starskiff appearing in Stargazer Navalia? It is not my first time hearing such a ghost story. Ever since I entered the Sky-Faring Commission, I've heard such legends every few decades. There're all different stories, yet every single one is detailed and convincing.

t_ch: 闲暇之余说些八卦闲谈无伤大雅，但这里是工作场合，应当谈论的当然是工作中的正事。
t_en: It's fine to talk about these rumors during leisure time, but this is a workplace. All discussions should only be related to work.

t_ch: 天舶司成员镇日里聊些荒诞不经的星槎流言，对工作有任何帮助吗？
t_en: Do you think it's useful for the Sky-Faring Commission members to discuss these nonsensical rumors about a starskiff all day long?

t_ch: （怎么办？驭空大人的回答真是无懈可击……）
t_en: (What should we do? Madam Yukong's answers are impeccable...)

t_ch: （咱们坦率一些不成吗？）
t_en: (Why don't we get honest with her?)

t_ch: 驭、驭空大人，我是十王司的判官…那个，我担心您已受到岁阳侵扰……
t_en: Ma-Madam Yukong, I'm a Judge of the Ten-Lords Commission... Well, I'm afraid that you have been affected by a heliobus...

t_ch: 司辰宫里行事多有不便，但我们想邀请您到绥园，好好做个检查。
t_en: As it's inconvenient for us to handle this matter in the Palace of Astrum, we would like to invite you to Fyxestroll Garden for a thorough check.

t_ch: 你们怀疑我被岁阳寄体了？
t_en: You suspect that I'm being possessed by a heliobus?

t_ch: 没错！
t_en: That's right!

t_ch: 其实我还不太确定…
t_en: Well, I'm not so sure myself...

t_ch: 岁、岁阳会被人强烈的情感所吸引，会根据你的经验和记忆产生幻觉……
t_en: A h—heliobus will be attracted by one's strong emotions and produce hallucinations based on your experiences and memories...

t_ch: 小妹妹，身为司舵，我早已被知会了这次岁阳灾异的来龙去脉，我知道它的特性。岁阳所生成的幻觉，我绝不会相信的……
t_en: Young lady, as the Helm Master, I've already been well-informed of the circumstances of the current heliobus disaster. I know its characteristics, and I will never trust the hallucinations the heliobi produce...

t_ch: 算了，开诚布公，才能尽解疑惑。身为司舵，我自当遵守十王判官的要求。
t_en: Never mind. Only when we are open and honest can we resolve all our doubts. As the Helm Master, I should comply with the requirements of a Judge of the Ten-Lords Commission.

t_ch: 不过，得等我忙完公务。入夜我会去绥园见你们，这样可以吗？
t_en: However, you'll have to wait until I'm done with work. I will meet you at Fyxestroll Garden tonight. Is that okay with you?

t_ch: 太、太好了。
t_en: C—Certainly.

t_ch: 唉，我也有很长的时间没去过狐眠冢了，咱们就在那儿见面吧。届时，各位所有的疑惑都将会得到解答。
t_en: Well, it's been a long time since I visited Foxsomn Tomb. Let's meet there. By then, all your doubts will be answered.

t_ch: 判官小姐，去做你们该做的事吧，我在这里待段时间就离开。
t_en: Miss judge, go to what you need to do. I will stay here for a while, then take my leave.

t_ch: 唉，我也有很长的时间没去过狐眠冢了，咱们就在那儿见面吧。
t_en: Well, it's been a long time since I visited Foxsomn Tomb. Let's meet there.

t_ch: 怎么样？她答应了吗？
t_en: How was it? Did she agree?

t_ch: 答应了。你妈妈，好威严，和…和我妈一样…一样的让人说不出话来。
t_en: Yes. Your Mom is very stern. J—Just like my Mom... Her presence makes it difficult to speak up.

t_ch: 没想到，你也有这样的妈妈。
t_en: I didn't expect you to have a Mom like this.

t_ch: 几位，我有种直觉，驭空大人没有被附身。
t_en: Everyone, my intuition is telling me that Madam Yukong isn't being possessed.

t_ch: 同心火铃没什么反应。
t_en: There isn't any reaction from the Harmonic Chime.

t_ch: 暂时不能排除这个可能…
t_en: We shouldn't leave out any possibilities...

t_ch: 真的吗？可她以前从不会流露出悲伤的表情，有时很恍惚，她会看着我，想说什么却没有说……
t_en: Are you serious? But in the past, she would never reveal such a melancholic expression. Sometimes, she becomes so distracted that she stares blankly at me without uttering a word...

t_ch: 忽然之间，她就像是需要我来哄睡的孩子，我也不知道怎么办，只能静静地搂着她。
t_en: It's like she suddenly turned into a child who needs me to put her to sleep. I don't know what to do either, so I can only embrace her silently in my arms.

t_ch: 连前阵子仙舟上出那么大的事儿她都没表现出一点脆弱的样子。但突然有一天，她回来时就变成了那样…我、我也想不出别的可能了。
t_en: We had such a huge crisis on the Xianzhou a short while ago, but she didn't show a single sign of weakness back then. Yet she came home one day and started to act in this way... I—I can't think of other possibilities.

t_ch: 可每个人都有柔弱的一面。
t_en: But everyone has a soft side.

t_ch: 是呀，越是强势的人，这一面藏得就越好，我娘就是这样的。
t_en: Exactly. The stronger the person appears to be, the better they are in concealing their soft side. That's how my Ma is.

t_ch: 蛊惑人心的幽灵星槎…
t_en: A phantom starskiff that puts a spell on people's hearts...

t_ch: 嗯，连晴霓小姐这样亲近的人都能察觉到她性情大变，多半是因为这古怪的事情吧。
t_en: Yeah, for someone as approachable as Miss Qingni to detect a change in her personality, it's likely due to an unnatural phenomenon.

t_ch: 不管怎样，驭空大人答应了今晚会在绥园与我们见面，到时候应该能问个清楚。
t_en: Regardless, Madam Yukong agreed to meet us at Fyxestroll Garden tonight. We should clear our doubts with her.

t_ch: 交给我们吧…不过，要是能近距离拍拍幽灵星槎就好了，那样——
t_en: Leave it to us... But it will be good if I can take a close-up picture of the phantom starskiff. That way —

t_ch: 「就肯定能涨粉了！」
t_en: (My fans will definitely increase!)

t_ch: 我就知道你在想什么。走，我们回绥园去吧。
t_en: I knew what you are up to. Come on. Let's go to Fyxestroll Garden.

t_ch: 驭空大人约定和我们在狐眠冢见面，时候差不多了吧？
t_en: Madam Yukong said to meet us at Foxsomn Tomb. Is it about time?

t_ch: 你们看！那是…晴霓小姐…的影子——？！
t_en: Look! Is that... Miss Qingni's... shadow!?
Ah —

t_ch: 有没有可能，是晴霓小姐被附身了？
t_en: Is it possible that Miss Qingni is the one actually being possessed?

t_ch: 总不能放着不管吧！
t_en: We can't ignore this!

t_ch: 附身是这么个特效吗？
t_en: Does possession produce that kind of visual special effects?

t_ch: 这时候就别插科打诨啦！追上去瞧瞧。
t_en: Now's not the time for jokes! Let's follow her.

t_ch: 走，追上去！
t_en: Come on. Let's catch up to her!

t_ch: 驭空…你会来吗……
t_en: Yukong... Will you come...

t_ch: 驭空…我们需要聊聊……
t_en: Yukong... We need to talk...

t_ch: 但…驭空还没来，你们又是谁？
t_en: But... Yukong isn't here yet. Who are you?

t_ch: 那是…幽灵星槎？！
t_en: Is that... the phantom starskiff!?

t_ch: 别想打扰我们……
t_en: Don't you dare disturb us...

t_ch: 这艘幽灵星槎的航行之旅，应该就到此结束了。
t_en: The voyage of the phantom starskiff should now come to an end.

t_ch: 驭空大人？
t_en: Madam Yukong?

t_ch: 嗯，我邀请你们来到狐眠冢，就是为了解除各位对幽灵星槎的疑惑。
t_en: Yes, I invited all of you to the Foxsomn Tomb so as to clear up any doubts you have about the phantom starskiff.

t_ch: 驭空…驭空，你终于来了。
t_en: Yukong... Yukong, you're finally here.

t_ch: 这个幻影，和晴霓小姐长得好像…她为什么一直在呼唤你的名字？
t_en: This illusion looks so much like Miss Qingni... Why is she constantly calling your name?

t_ch: 因为在它看来，它认得我，它…很想念我。
t_en: Because, as far as it is concerned, it recognizes me. It... misses me.

t_ch: 幽灵星槎…是岁阳因人们的思念而有灵具现。它循着那些哀悼人们的思绪，在仙舟上无定来去。
t_en: The phantom starskiff... is a manifestation of people's thoughts by the heliobus. It travels unpredictably on the Xianzhou, following the sorrowful thoughts of those who mourn.

t_ch: 当心怀悲伤的人来到狐眠冢，它就像听到了老朋友内心的哭泣声变化成了故人的样子来相见…来响应那些没能履行的重逢约定。
t_en: When someone with a broken heart comes to Foxsomn Tomb, it would come and meet them in the guise of the ones who had passed away, as if hearkening to the inner cries of their friend... It will respond to those unfulfilled promises of reunion.

t_ch: 狐眠冢有什么特别之处？
t_en: What's special about Foxsomn Tomb?

t_ch: 罗浮仙舟上少有祭奠的场所，但狐眠冢不同。它是狐人追思故人的地方。
t_en: There are few places on Xianzhou Luofu where one can honor the dead, but the Foxsomn Tomb is different. It's a place for foxians to commemorate the deceased.

t_ch: 三十年前与丰饶民的大战结束后，许多人都曾来到这儿纪念阵亡的飞行士。
t_en: After the great war with the Denizens of Abundance thirty years ago, many people had come here to commemorate the fallen pilots.

t_ch: 故人的样子？
t_en: Ones who had passed away?

t_ch: 它变化成了我阵亡的战友，采翼的模样。
t_en: It has taken form of my fallen comrade-in-arms, Caiyi.

t_ch: 这些年里，我无数次来到狐眠冢，怀念和采翼无话不说的日子，怀念与她心有灵犀的飞行，怀念和她并肩战斗的热血…我甚至将那场大战的飞舰残片收殓在这儿。
t_en: I have come to Foxsomn Tomb countless times over the years to reminisce my days with Caiyi. Back then, we told each other everything, we understood each other without needing words as we flew, and we fought passionately side by side... I even entombed the airship fragments from that great war here.

t_ch: 没想到时光流转，思念变成了星槎，它真的飞回来了。
t_en: I didn't expect that, as time went on, my thoughts for her would take the form of a starskiff and actually fly back to me.

t_ch: 你许久没有回到狐眠冢了，但你的悲伤却如此强烈。我…只好来找你。
t_en: You haven't been to Foxsomn Tomb for a long time, but your sadness only grows ever greater. I... had to come and find you.

t_ch: 我以为自己已经习惯不再怀念了，直到我走进船舱，再次见到你，我又想起了逝去的那些日子，我又变回了软弱的狐人姑娘，只会哭泣。
t_en: I thought I'd gotten used to not thinking of you. But when I boarded the starskiff and saw you again, I remembered those bygone days and was reduced to a weak foxian girl once more, a little kit who could do nothing but cry.

t_ch: 我品尝得到…你咸涩的眼泪，在心里流…一直在流。
t_en: I can taste your salty tears... you have never stopped crying in your heart...

t_ch: 你需要人聊聊，我会听你一直聊下去……
t_en: If you need to talk, I will always be here to talk with you...

t_ch: 也许我们应该给你俩留出空间…
t_en: Perhaps we should give you two some personal space...

t_ch: 驭空大人，你知道那只是幻象…
t_en: Madam Yukong, you should know that it's only an illusion...

t_ch: 我明白。
t_en: I know.

t_ch: 很抱歉。采翼，我叫十王司判官来这里…是为了和你告别。
t_en: I'm sorry, Caiyi. I've asked a Judge of the Ten-Lords Commission to come here... so I came to bid goodbye to you.

t_ch: 帝弓垂怜，能再次见到记忆中的你，真好。但是，我不能驻足在这一刻，我仍要带着对你的记忆往前进。
t_en: The Reignbow took pity on me, allowing me to once again meet the person you are in my memories. For that, I'm grateful. But I must not stay stagnant in this moment. I want to move on with my life while holding on to my memories of you.

t_ch: 你的女儿，不…是我们的女儿，晴霓，她很好。
t_en: Your daughter, no... Our daughter, Qingni, is doing very well now.

t_ch: 真抱歉啊，我不能完成对你的承诺了…晴霓会如她自己所选，成为一名飞行士。和你一样了不起的飞行士！
t_en: I'm sorry that I couldn't fulfill my promise to you... Qingni will become a pilot, as she chose for herself. She will become a pilot as marvelous as you were!

t_ch: 我明白，谢谢你。在飞去下一处前，我想问你……
t_en: I understand. Thank you. Before going to my next destination, I wish to ask you...

t_ch: 你…还会触碰天空吗？
t_en: Will you... ever touch the sky again?

t_ch: 驭空没有回话，她深深看向自己故友的幻影，把答案留在了心里。
t_en: Yukong doesn't reply. She gazes soulfully at the illusion of her deceased friend and keeps the answer to herself.

t_ch: 我明白了。再见，小空。
t_en: I see. Goodbye, Little Kong.

t_ch: …再见。
t_en: ...Goodbye.

t_ch: 幽灵与星槎突然散作飞舞的流萤星火，不再成形。你们将这些无主的岁阳收进了藏月瓠中。
t_en: The phantom and the starskiff suddenly disperse into fluttering glitters like fireflies, no longer taking shape. Your group captures these masterless heliobi into the Lunaumbra Gourd.

t_ch: 各位，我会向晴霓讲讲这几天发生的一切，她应该知道的。
t_en: Everyone, I will explain to Qingni about everything that happened in the past few days. She has the right to know.

t_ch: 等等，咱们没提过晴霓吧？
t_en: Hold on. We didn't mention anything about Qingni.

t_ch: 当女儿的有什么事能瞒得住妈妈呢。
t_en: Do you think a daughter can hide anything from her mother?

t_ch: 判官小姐，岁阳已经被收服了。我想，你们的任务应该也完成了。
t_en: Miss Judge, the heliobi have been captured. I believe your mission here is complete.

t_ch: 驭空大人……
t_en: Madam Yukong...

t_ch: 请容我一个人在这儿静静待一会儿。
t_en: I would like to have some time alone now.

t_ch: 思念变成一艘星槎飞回来了，原来岁阳也会做这样的事……
t_en: A person's thoughts for someone could take the form of a starskiff and return. I see, so heliobi are even capable of such a thing...

t_ch: …在这种时候，我才会回想起寒鸦大人说，岁阳只是被情绪吸引的星火之精。
t_en: ...Times like this make me recall how Madam Hanya said heliobi are simply starfire essence attracted by emotions.

t_ch: 也许，岁阳从来没有基于善恶而决定行动。
t_en: Perhaps the heliobi never acted based on concepts such as good or evil.

t_ch: 有道理……
t_en: You have a point...

t_ch: 那个，虽然有些破坏气氛……
t_en: Well, I know this spoils the mood...

t_ch: 你说吧。
t_en: Just say it.

t_ch: 我拍到了幽灵星槎，咱们现在发个帖子吧？
t_en: I got a picture of the phantom starskiff. How about making a post right now?

t_ch: 确实破坏气氛，我来发吧。
t_en: Yes, that spoils the mood, but let me post it.

t_ch: 涨粉还得仰仗你。
t_en: We gotta count on you to increase our fan numbers.

t_ch: 嘿嘿。
t_en: Heehee.

t_ch: …我猜，驭空大人之后还会回来的吧。
t_en: ...I believe Madam Yukong will still return to this place in the future.

t_ch: 那今天就这样解散吧，回见！
t_en: That's all for today. See you all later!

t_ch: 请看，这是真正的幽灵星槎…投影。
t_en: Look, this is the real phantom starskiff's... projection.

t_ch: 哎，大费周章。
t_en: Ack, all for nothing.

t_ch: 狐人的奠仪会将逝者们的遗物放上星槎，送出仙舟，航入恒星，与之同辉。
t_en: The foxian ceremony will place items of the dead onto the starskiff, sending them out of Xianzhou and into the stars to shine brilliantly among them.

t_ch: …其实是希望故人乘船归来吧？
t_en: ...But in fact, we are hoping the dead will board the starskiff and return home, no?

t_ch: 我和天舶司的同僚商量了一番，我们在狐眠冢放置星槎投影，让每一个前来悼念的人心中有所寄托。妈妈，你看看，这怎么样？
t_en: I had a discussion with our colleagues in the Sky-Faring Commission, and we decided to place a projection of the starskiff at Foxsomn Tomb, so everyone who comes to mourn will have something to pin their hopes on. Mother, take a look at this. What do you think?

t_ch: 做得好，就让它停靠在这里吧。
t_en: Well done. We'll keep it docked at Foxsomn Tomb.

t_ch: 是你啊，{NICKNAME}。这次幽灵星槎的事麻烦你了，替我向捉鬼小队表示感谢。
t_en: It's you, {NICKNAME}. Thanks for your help with the phantom starskiff this time. Please help me thank the ghost-hunting squad as well.

t_ch: 我也要谢谢你们。
t_en: I would like to thank you and your team too.

t_ch: 不用谢，这是我们应该做的。
t_en: You're welcome. That's our job.

t_ch: 嗯，辛苦你们了。
t_en: Understood. Thank you for your hard work.

t_ch: 谢什么谢，十王司的任务罢了。
t_en: Don't mention it. We're just completing a job for the Ten-Lords Commission.

t_ch: 你谦虚的方式总是有些奇怪。
t_en: You always have a strange way of being humble.

t_ch: …所以为什么一开始要禁止天舶司谈论幽灵星槎啊？
t_en: ...So why did you forbid Sky-Faring Commission staff from talking about the phantom starskiff?

t_ch: 谁知道一传十、十传百之后会变成什么恐怖传说呢？
t_en: Words spread like wildfire. Who knows what kind of horrible legend it will end up as?

t_ch: …有点道理。
t_en: ...Fair enough.

t_ch: 我们先行一步了。
t_en: We'll take our leave first.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}再见。
t_en: Goodbye, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 呜呜，我的照片…这下全完了……
t_en: Boohoo, my photos... They're done for...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}！你总算来了，快帮帮我——
t_en: {NICKNAME}! You're finally here. Help me out, quick...

t_ch: 你怎么了？
t_en: What's wrong?

t_ch: 唉，我遇到了一件倒霉事……
t_en: Ahh, I got myself into a real pickle...

t_ch: 去哪？打谁？
t_en: Where do you want me to go? Who do I beat up?

t_ch: 诶，冷静点啦，这次不是去打架！
t_en: Jeez, calm down. We're not going to fight anyone this time!

t_ch: 回见…
t_en: See ya later...

t_ch: 喂喂，别这么绝情呀，你先听我说完嘛！
t_en: Hey, don't be so merciless. At least listen to what I have to say!

t_ch: 我今天原本计划整理一下相机里的相片，结果……
t_en: I was planning on sorting my photos in the camera today, but...

t_ch: …结果我发现，这些照片里全都是奇怪的黑影！你快看！
t_en: ...But I realized that these photos — all of them — have this weird shadow in it! Check it out!

t_ch: 之前旅途中拍的好多照片都报废了，太让咱心疼了……
t_en: Lots of photos I took during the trips got ruined thanks to this. Ouch, that really hurts...

t_ch: 以后再拍新的吧。
t_en: Take some new photos later.

t_ch: 不行，这些都是独一无二的回忆！
t_en: No, those were all unique memories!

t_ch: 这就是灵异照片吧…
t_en: These are supernatural photos, no...?

t_ch: 才不是呢，你不要吓我啊！
t_en: No way, don't freak me out!

t_ch: 是你拍照技术的问题。
t_en: Sounds like a skill issue.

t_ch: 说什么呢！技术再不好也不至于拍出这种黑影吧？
t_en: What do you mean! No matter how bad my photography is, it wouldn't produce these shadows, would it?

t_ch: 我问过丹恒原因，他满嘴都是「跃迁辐射」、「太空强磁场」、「虚数影尘」这种听不懂的词儿，总之就是说照片数据全都受损了……
t_en: I asked Dan Heng about it. He went off about all these unintelligible things like "warp jump radiation," "space magnetic fields," "imaginary void dust." Anyway, what it means is that all of the photo data has been damaged...

t_ch: 这些都是咱无名客在旅程中的珍贵回忆，可不能说不要就不要。
t_en: Those are all precious memories of us Nameless. We can't simply say we don't want them.

t_ch: 事已至此，我只想回到以往拍摄的地点补拍一些照片。但数量实在太多了，我一个人根本拍不完……
t_en: Now I just want to go back to where I took those pics and re-take them, but there are far too many for me to take by myself...

t_ch: 所以…我相信乐于助人的{NICKNAME}一定会帮我的，对吧？
t_en: So... I'm sure the helpful {NICKNAME} will give me a hand, right?

t_ch: 好，我这就去！
t_en: Alright, let's go!

t_ch: 你先别急啊，我还没说要怎么拍呢！
t_en: Relax, I haven't said how to take those pics yet!

t_ch: 怎么还要我干活…
t_en: Why do I have to work...

t_ch: 因为这些照片都是和你一起拍的呀，也算是你自己的旅途的珍贵记录嘛~
t_en: Because all of these photos were taken with you, so it's kind of a precious memory of your own journey too~

t_ch: 我有什么好处？
t_en: What's in it for me?

t_ch: 你能重温故地的美好回忆，借机学习摄影技术，顺便还收获了美少女的感谢——这还不够吗？
t_en: You can relive beautiful memories of your travels, use this chance to learn about photography, and also earn the gratitude of a beautiful girl — isn't that enough?

t_ch: 好，就这么决定啦！我把受损的相片和大致的拍摄区域发给你，你去重新拍一张类似的照片就行！
t_en: Alright, it's decided then! I'll send you the damaged photos and the approximate location they were taken in, and you just take a similar pic there!

t_ch: 不过这些照片的内容都被黑影覆盖了，具体拍的是什么内容，咱也认不太出来了…就靠火眼金睛的{NICKNAME}你自己判别吧，嘿嘿～
t_en: But the subjects of these photos have all been obscured by the shadows, so I'm not too sure about what exactly each photo's about... So let's just rely on the penetrating insight of {NICKNAME} to judge once and for all, teehee~

t_ch: 我先挑了一张在列车上拍的受损相片。你可以先尝试一下，有不明白的再问我吧～
t_en: I picked out one aboard the Express as our starting point. You can try it first and come find me if you have any questions~

t_ch: 这张照片虽说也有黑影，但应该不难看出拍的是什么吧？
t_en: Despite all the black shadow on this photo, it shouldn't be too hard to see what it is, right?

t_ch: 拍完记得给我看看，等你哦！
t_en: Don't forget to show me the photos. I'm waiting!

t_ch: 我忘了…
t_en: I forgot...

t_ch: 来来来，我教你！先把拍摄主体放进取景框里，相机会有目标识别提示的——接下来按下快门就好啦！
t_en: Come on, let me show you! First, line up the subject in the viewfinder. There's a target recognition thingy — then just push the shutter button!

t_ch: 拍完照片记得给我看看，等你哦！
t_en: Don't forget to show me the photos. I'm waiting!

t_ch: 诶？{NICKNAME}乘客是在认真记录列车长工作的样子吗？帕姆好开心～
t_en: Huh? Is {NICKNAME} diligently recording the conductor's every move? Pom-Pom is happy~

t_ch: 你看这照片能行吗？
t_en: How's this shot?

t_ch: 我看看…不错嘛，{NICKNAME}，你很有摄影天赋诶。
t_en: Let me see... Not bad, {NICKNAME}. You've got quite the knack for photography.

t_ch: 那这些照片就拜托你啦，拍完就用短信发给我吧，我会准备谢礼的！
t_en: Then I'll leave these photos to you. Send me them via message when you're done, and I'll give you a present~

t_ch: 等等，你刚刚在拍什么？
t_en: Hang on, what were you just filming then?

t_ch: 拍的是佩佩
t_en: Just snapping a photo of Peppy
Oh...

t_ch: 拍的是风景
t_en: Just snapping a scenery pic

t_ch: 抱歉，空间站中有许多对外保密的研究，我需要检查一下你的照片。
t_en: Sorry, there's a lot of confidential research being undertaken aboard the space station. I'm going to have to check those photos.

t_ch: 你这拍的不是佩佩吗？
t_en: Is this a photo of Peppy?

t_ch: 拍的是阿兰
t_en: Just snapping a pic of you

t_ch: 虽然我不在意，但拍摄理应经过别人同意，请让我看看你的照片。
t_en: Look, I don't mind, but photography should be done with the subject's consent. Please show me your photos.

t_ch: 你这拍的不是佩佩吗？
t_en: Is this a photo of Peppy?

t_ch: 那个…这张照片能发我一份吗？
t_en: Then... can you please send me a copy of this photo?

t_ch: 你好呀，{NICKNAME}，有什么事吗？
t_en: Hello, {NICKNAME}. What's going on?

t_ch: 我想给你拍张照片。
t_en: I'd like to take a picture of you.

t_ch: 当然可以，不过我对自己照片的要求可不低，你得认真拍才行哦。
t_en: Of course, no problem. But I have high standards about my image, so you'll have to take it seriously.

t_ch: 我看看…很厉害嘛，{NICKNAME}！你的技术不比我的私家摄影师差呀~
t_en: Let me see... Brilliant, {NICKNAME}! You're right up there with my own regular personal photographer~

t_ch: 这张照片你就留着吧，发出去之前记得修图。
t_en: Hold onto this photo yourself. Don't forget to edit it before you send it out.

t_ch: 定分枪突然睁开了眼，转了转眼珠，盯着你的相机屏幕——
t_en: The Rating Pistol suddenly opened its eye, rolled its pupil over several times, and then locked onto your camera screen:

t_ch: 「{TEXTJOIN#72}{TEXTJOIN#73}分！」定分枪突然宣布，不知道是不是你照片的分数。
t_en: "{TEXTJOIN#72}{TEXTJOIN#73} points!" the Rating Pistol suddenly proclaims. It was unsure if that was meant to be the score of your photos or otherwise.

t_ch: 嗯？你在这干吗？
t_en: Hmm? What are you doing?

t_ch: 我想给你拍张照片。
t_en: I'd like to take a picture of you.

t_ch: 拍呗，随便你。
t_en: Snap away, whatever.

t_ch: …快拍啊？
t_en: ...Make it quick, will you?

t_ch: 我想拍三个黑塔的合照。
t_en: I'd like to take a picture of three of you, Herta.

t_ch: 奇怪的要求…不过想法还算有点意思。
t_en: That's a strange request... Though it is intriguing...

t_ch: 能再叫两个遥控人偶过来吗？
t_en: Can you please get two more remote-control puppets to come over?

t_ch: 要求真多，行吧，别浪费我太多时间。
t_en: That's a lot to ask. Fine, but don't waste too much of my time.

t_ch: 我需要更多黑塔。
t_en: I need more of Herta.
...Huh?

t_ch: 你是需要遥控人偶吧，说清楚好吗？
t_en: You need some remote-control puppets, right? Can't you just say so?

t_ch: 我把闲置的遥控人偶弄过来，等着。
t_en: I'll bring over a couple of idle puppets. Hold on.

t_ch: 黑塔操纵来了两具遥控人偶……
t_en: Herta manipulated two remote-control puppets to come over...

t_ch: 快拍，操控三个人偶好麻烦。
t_en: Quick, take the pic. It's tricky operating three of these at once.

t_ch: 让我看看。
t_en: Let me see.

t_ch: 能把我拍得这么普通也算一种本事，下次不许拍了。
t_en: It must take real skill to make me look so mundane. No more photos next time.

t_ch: 呜呜，呜呜呜——
t_en: Woo-woo, wubba-woo...

t_ch: 呜呜伯看了看你的相机屏幕，似乎对你的照片很感兴趣。
t_en: Wubbaboo looks at your camera screen and seems to be interested in your photos.

t_ch: 呜呜呜！
t_en: Woo-woo woo!

t_ch: 呜呜伯似乎想要你再拍一张。
t_en: Wubbaboo apparently wants you to take another photo.

t_ch: 再拍一张。
t_en: Take another photo.

t_ch: 呜呜，呜呜呜！
t_en: Woo-woo, woo whoooo woo!

t_ch: 不满足的呜呜伯似乎想要你再拍一张。
t_en: Wubbaboo, apparently still unsatisfied, wants you to take another photo.

t_ch: 不拍了。
t_en: Don't take it.

t_ch: 你收起了相机，呜呜伯似乎在表达不满。
t_en: You pack up your camera and the Wubbaboo seems displeased.

t_ch: （差不多得了，不要浪费相机内存了……）
t_en: (Pretty much done. Don't want to waste too much camera memory...)

t_ch: 呜呜…呜呜呜呜呜呜！
t_en: Woo-woo, woo whoooo wubba-woo!

t_ch: 你收起了相机，呜呜伯似乎在表达不满。
t_en: You pack up your camera and the Wubbaboo seems displeased.

t_ch: （拍摄的目标似乎是十分巨大的东西……）
t_en: (The photo subject seems to be something enormous...)

t_ch: （奇怪，照片上为什么会有呜呜伯？）
t_en: (Strange, why is a Wubbaboo in this photo?)

t_ch: （看来它们真的很喜欢拍照……）
t_en: (Looks like they really love photography...)

t_ch: 诶？你刚才是在拍我吗？
t_en: Huh? Were you just taking a photo of me?

t_ch: 太好了，终于有人发现我的才华了，不枉我天天坐在这里卖唱！你是艺术记者吗？
t_en: Awesome! Finally, somebody noticed my talent. I knew it wouldn't be a waste, busking here on the street every day! Are you a reporter of the arts?

t_ch: 我是路人。
t_en: I'm just passing by.

t_ch: 啊？只是路人啊…真是的，白高兴一场。
t_en: Oh, just passing by... Really, I got excited about nothing.

t_ch: 这次就算了，下次拍照要给小费哦。
t_en: Don't worry about it this time, but the next photo will cost.

t_ch: 我是你的粉丝。
t_en: I'm one of your fans.

t_ch: 原来只是粉…等等，我已经有粉丝了吗？！
t_en: So you're just a fan... Hang on, I have fans!?

t_ch: 看来我离出名也不远了——这份礼物，请你收下！感谢你的支持，我会继续努力的！
t_en: Looks like I'm right on the cusp of becoming famous... Here you go, take this gift! Thank you for your support, I'll keep at it!

t_ch: 我是记者，你要火了。
t_en: I'm a reporter. You're the next big thing.

t_ch: 真、真的吗？请一定要多分我一些版面！终于熬出头了！
t_en: R—Really? Please give me a centerfold spread! I've finally hit the big time!

t_ch: 你准备掏出手机，给三月七发送今天的照片……
t_en: You get ready to pull out your phone and send today's photos to March 7th...

t_ch: 你突然感受到了一股巨大的冲击，眼前一片漆黑……
t_en: You suddenly feel the force of a massive impact, and everything goes dark...

t_ch: …你想起来了，在危险拍摄的过程中，你被行驶中的叮叮车撞到了路边。
t_en: ...You suddenly recall that during a dangerous photo-taking session that you got hit by a moving tram.

t_ch: 你还能醒来本身就是个奇迹，而叮叮车的铃声仿佛是它对你的斥责——
t_en: The fact you're still alive is already a miracle, and the tram's bell seems to be scolding you...

t_ch: 「会不会走路？长没长眼睛？交通规则懂不懂啊？啊？」
t_en: "Do you know how to cross the street? Are you blind? Don't you know anything about traffic rules? Huh?"

t_ch: …下次别再干这种事了，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: ...Don't do that again, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 喂！荣誉队员，快帮帮虎克！
t_en: Hey, honorary member! Quick, help me out!

t_ch: 虎克只是到外面冒险了一会儿，这边的门就被锁上了，快放我进来！
t_en: I only just popped out for a minute but I locked myself out. Let me in!

t_ch: 放虎克进来。
t_en: Let Hook in.

t_ch: 呼——总算回来了，多亏了荣誉队员！
t_en: Whew — I'm back. Thanks a lot, honorary member!

t_ch: 看你拿着相机，是想要记录虎克大人的英姿吗？那就尽情拍吧，这是你救下虎克的奖赏！
t_en: That's a nice camera. Do you want to capture the mighty figure of Pitch-Dark Hook the Great? Go ahead, that's your prize for saving me!

t_ch: 直接拍照。
t_en: Take a pic.

t_ch: 喂喂喂，荣誉队员，你拿着相机要干什么？快放我进来！
t_en: Hey hey hey, honorary member. What are you doing with that camera? Let me in!

t_ch: 哼哼，用这份照片好好宣扬虎克大人的威名吧！
t_en: Hmph, let this photo show to all the fierceness of Pitch-Dark Hook the Great!

t_ch: 可恶，你居然趁机拍下虎克大人出丑的样子，给虎克等着！
t_en: Unbelievable! You actually took the opportunity to capture Pitch Dark Hook the Great's embarrassing moment. Just you wait!

t_ch: 虎克从铁门上爬了下来……
t_en: Hook climbs out the gate...

t_ch: 呼…呼……好累，这次就先算了，下次一定会按鼹鼠党的规矩惩罚你！
t_en: Whew... huff... I'm pooped. You win this one, but next time we'll punish you for breaking the rules of The Moles!

t_ch: 哟，{NICKNAME}，要买张今晚比赛的票吗？
t_en: Hey, {NICKNAME}. Wanna buy a ticket to tonight's match?

t_ch: 诶，{NICKNAME}呀，想给我拍照也用不着这么见外吧？
t_en: Hey, {NICKNAME}, you really don't need to be so formal when taking pictures for me.

t_ch: 唉，老桑博确实是在磐岩镇越来越受欢迎了，但在别人工作的时候可不能随便拍……
t_en: Yo, looks like your ol' Sampo is starting to get more and more famous around Boulder Town. But you can't just take pictures of people when they're working...

t_ch: 既然是你想要我的照片，我老桑博也不是完全不讲人情…这样吧，<unbreak>50</unbreak>信用点！想怎么拍就怎么拍，合照也行！
t_en: But since you want my photo, I'm not completely unreasonable... Well, how about <unbreak>50</unbreak> credits!? You can shoot it any way you want — even a selfie with me!

t_ch: 我没拍你。
t_en: I wasn't trying to photograph you.

t_ch: 跟我害羞什么呀，来来，让我看看……
t_en: Don't be coy with me. C'mon, let me see...

t_ch: 你看，我这不是出镜了吗？
t_en: Look, I'm in the shot, aren't I?

t_ch: 我很理解你的仰慕之情，但如果给你开了这个先例，今后的工作可就麻烦了，我这行可不能天天被相机对着啊。
t_en: I understand your admiration, but if I make this exception for you, then it'll make things hard for me later on. In this line of work, I can't just get caught on film anywhere, y'know.

t_ch: （交出信用点）
t_en: (Pay the credits)

t_ch: 嘿嘿，谢谢惠顾~
t_en: Heehee, you're too kind. Thanks!

t_ch: 我会把照片交给地火的。
t_en: I'll pass the photos on to Wildfire.

t_ch: 哎哟，别别别，上次因为倒票这事，卢卡小兄弟差点给我一拳！
t_en: No no, don't do that. Luka nearly punched my lights out last time for scalping tickets.

t_ch: 有话好好说，以后老桑博随便你拍，好不好？
t_en: Hey, we can talk about this. How about this: You can take photos of me whenever you like in the future, alright?

t_ch: 呃…看来是误会。不过你真的不想要我的照片吗？免费也不要？
t_en: Err... Looks like there's been a misunderstanding. But are you sure you don't need a photo of me? Not even a free one?

t_ch: 下次再拍。
t_en: Next time.

t_ch: 好吧，但错过了这次就要收费了哦！
t_en: Alright, but next time it's gonna cost you!

t_ch: 给钱都不拍。
t_en: Not even if you paid me.

t_ch: 唉，你好狠心，真是太让老桑博心寒了……
t_en: Ouch, that's harsh, friend. You don't have to be so mean to Sampo...

t_ch: 这些看电视的机兵挡着你需要拍摄的照片……
t_en: These automatons watching TV are blocking the photo you need to take...

t_ch: 电视，好看。
t_en: TV, good.

t_ch: （劝说它们离开）
t_en: (Persuade them to leave)

t_ch: 你对机兵们解释了你的来意，但它似乎并不理解。
t_en: You try explaining your intentions with the automatons, but they don't seem to understand.

t_ch: 电视，在看，人类，等等。
t_en: TV, watching. Human, wait.

t_ch: （把电视关掉）
t_en: (Turn off the TV)

t_ch: 电视，没了，无聊，无聊。
t_en: TV, gone. Boring, boring.

t_ch: 机兵们不满地离开了……
t_en: The automatons leave in disappointment...

t_ch: （等它们自行离开）
t_en: (Wait for them to leave by themselves)

t_ch: 等了很久，机兵们终于满足地离开了……
t_en: After a long time, the automatons finally leave in contentment...

t_ch: （把电视关掉）
t_en: (Turn off the TV)

t_ch: 电视，没了，无聊，无聊。
t_en: TV, gone. Boring, boring.

t_ch: 机兵们不满地离开了……
t_en: The automatons leave in disappointment...

t_ch: （等它们自行离开）
t_en: (Wait for them to leave by themselves)

t_ch: 等了很久，机兵们终于满足地离开了……
t_en: After a long time, the automatons finally leave in contentment...

t_ch: （滑梯上好像有两只扑满……）
t_en: (Seems like there's a couple of Trotters on the slide...)

t_ch: 扑满被你的快门声吓到了，吸走了你的照片……
t_en: The Trotter is surprised by the sound of your shutter, and sucks your photo away...

t_ch: （照片不在它嘴里，身上也没有好东西，真是只穷酸的扑满……）
t_en: (The photo isn't in its mouth, and there's nothing valuable on its body. It really is a poor, pitiful Trotter...)

t_ch: 扑满趴在地上瑟瑟发抖，它现在已经无路可逃了。
t_en: The Trotter lies on the ground shaking. There's no place for it to escape to now.

t_ch: 把照片还给我！
t_en: Give me back the photo!

t_ch: 扑满似乎听懂了你的话，把照片吐了出来便逃走了……
t_en: The Trotter seems to understand you, and spits out the photo before fleeing...

t_ch: 只能把你干掉了！
t_en: I'll just have to take you out!

t_ch: 扑满似乎看到了你的杀意，慌忙逃跑，照片掉在了路上……
t_en: The Trotter seems to have sensed your bloodthirst, and flees in a hurry, dropping the photo in the middle of the street...

t_ch: （亲手从它身上找照片）
t_en: (Shake it down for the photo with your own two hands)

t_ch: 你撬开了扑满的嘴巴，看到它如黑洞般深邃的咽喉，慢慢抚摸它光滑的肚皮，轻轻按压……
t_en: You pry open the Trotter's mouth to see its deep, dark gullet akin to a black hole. You slowly stroke its shiny belly, and softly press on it...

t_ch: 扑满挣扎着吐出了你的照片，仓皇地逃走了……
t_en: The Trotter struggles and spits out your photo, then flees in a panic...

t_ch: （似乎在列车里抬头的时候看到过…）
t_en: (Think I saw this when I looked up on the Express...)

t_ch: （还差最后一张照片，总感觉这个人影很眼熟……）
t_en: (Just one more pic. This person seems familiar to me...)

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，拍照的进展怎么样啦？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, how are the photos coming along?

t_ch: 还差最后一张…
t_en: Just one more to go...

t_ch: 太好了，那就让咱帮你看看最后一张是什么吧！
t_en: Fantastic! Then let's see who the final photo is of!

t_ch: 嗯…看起来好眼熟啊……
t_en: Hmm... This looks really familiar...

t_ch: 好像是你。
t_en: It looks like you.

t_ch: 啊，对啊，这不就是我吗！
t_en: Ah, yes. Isn't this me?

t_ch: 看不出来是谁。
t_en: I can't tell who it is.

t_ch: 我觉得…这好像是我的自拍诶！
t_en: This looks like... a selfie of me!

t_ch: 应该是只扑满。
t_en: I think it's a Trotter.

t_ch: 怎么可能是只扑满啊！这应该是本姑娘吧！
t_en: What do you mean a Trotter! That's clearly me!

t_ch: 如果只是自拍的话，好像也没什么特别的价值……
t_en: If it's just a selfie, it can't be that meaningful...

t_ch: 有了，那这张照片就由你来拍吧，这样就是不一样的回忆了！
t_en: I know, why don't you take this photo then? Then it becomes a whole new memory!

t_ch: 经过这么多天的练习，你应该已经很熟练了吧？记得要拍得可爱一点哦！
t_en: After all these days of practice, you should be pretty good by now, right? Make sure I look cute, okay?

t_ch: 来来来，让咱看看！
t_en: Quick, quick, let's have a look!
Oh...

t_ch: …真好看，本姑娘果然是天生丽质！
t_en: ...That looks great! I truly am a natural beauty!

t_ch: 还不是我拍得好。
t_en: So it's not because of my photography skills?

t_ch: 好好好~看在你帮我这么多忙的份上，我就承认这点吧。
t_en: Alright, alright~ As you've helped me so much, I'll give you that.

t_ch: 确实很好看。
t_en: It really is a great photo.

t_ch: 对吧！你的审美也算是过关啦！
t_en: Right? Looks like your taste is passable, too!

t_ch: 不怎么好看。
t_en: It doesn't look that great.

t_ch: 最上等的食材往往不需要复杂的烹饪工序…就算你的拍照技术还差点火候，凭咱这张脸也足以拯救这张照片啦！
t_en: The finest ingredients don't require complex recipes... Even if your photography skills leave a lot to be desired, at least my beautiful face was enough to save this photo!

t_ch: 之前真是吓死我了，还以为这些照片再也找不回来了呢。
t_en: I was so scared that I was never going to be able to get these photos back.

t_ch: 总感觉如果失去了这些记录，这些回忆也会被淡忘…但肯定都是错觉！本姑娘可不会忘记我们一起经历的旅程！
t_en: I felt like if I lost these records, these memories would just fade away... But, that's not true! There is no way I would forget our journeys together!

t_ch: 不管怎么说，总算是把咱的照片全都救回来了，这次的事情也可以算是旅途中的珍贵回忆之一了！
t_en: Either way, we finally managed to get all of our photos back. Now, this whole ordeal will become another precious memory along our journey!

t_ch: 所以说…{NICKNAME}，我们拍张合照留念吧！
t_en: So... Let's take a photo together to commemorate this moment, {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: （拍摄合照）
t_en: (Take a photo together)

t_ch: 好啦！快看，怎么样？
t_en: Alright! What do you think?

t_ch: 不愧是三月七。
t_en: You never fail to impress!

t_ch: 哼哼，多谢夸奖~
t_en: Hehe, thanks for the praise~

t_ch: 我果然天生丽质！
t_en: I truly am a natural beauty!

t_ch: 喂，你不要学我啊！
t_en: Hey! Don't copy me!

t_ch: 感觉拍得不够好。
t_en: It doesn't look great.

t_ch: 唉，练习了这么多天，你的艺术素养居然还没有提升……
t_en: Ah, after all these days of practice, your artistic skills still haven't improved...

t_ch: 好了，那这张照片就送给你吧！
t_en: Alright, I'll give you this one!

t_ch: 喏，好好保管，不要弄丢或者弄坏了……
t_en: Take care of it, okay? Don't lose it or damage it...

t_ch: 最重要的是——不要忘了这是我给你拍的照片！
t_en: Most importantly... Don't forget that I gave you this picture!

t_ch: 拍摄的目标应该不是桑博，再拍一张吧……
t_en: Sampo probably isn't the target for this picture, let's take another one...

t_ch: （拍摄的角度不太好，还是再拍一张吧……）
t_en: (The angle isn't great on this one, I should take another one...)

t_ch: 没问题！
t_en: No problem!

t_ch: 喂喂，大名鼎鼎的开拓者不会想耍赖吧？
t_en: Hey, hey! The great Trailblazer wouldn't be up to anything shameless now, right?

t_ch: 没什么。
t_en: It's nothing.

t_ch: 怎么了？忘了使用相机的方法了吗？
t_en: What's wrong? Have you forgotten how to use a camera?

t_ch: 怎么样，拍完了吗？
t_en: How's it going? Have you finished taking pictures?

t_ch: 演出结束，周围的人群散去了…
t_en: The performance finishes and the surrounding crowd disperses...

t_ch: （照片里的人应该就在附近……）
t_en: (The person in the photo must be nearby...)

t_ch: （照片里的东西应该要等等才能出现……）
t_en: (I need to wait for the object in the picture to appear...)

t_ch: （怎么还不来…再等一会儿吧……）
t_en: (What's taking so long... I'll wait a little longer...)

t_ch: （照片里的黑影应该是列车上常常会见到的东西……）
t_en: (The shadow in the photo should be something commonly seen on the Express...)

t_ch: 没问题！
t_en: No problem!

t_ch: 三月七把你们的合照放到了照片墙
t_en: March 7th put your photo on the photo wall.

t_ch: 你把虎克放了进来…
t_en: You moved Hook into the picture...

t_ch: 目标对象被遮挡！
t_en: Target is blocked!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，这边，这边！
t_en: Hey, {NICKNAME}, over here!

t_ch: {F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}，我隔着老远就看到你啦，你要去金人巷吗？
t_en: You're easy to spot, haha! Are you going to Aurum Alley?

t_ch: 没错。
t_en: Indeed I am.

t_ch: 那像你这样刚刚抵达仙舟的旅客一定很需要一个导游吧！
t_en: A new arrival like you should get a tour guide!

t_ch: 好啊。
t_en: Seems fair.

t_ch: 嗯嗯，作为到处吃喝…咳，外出看诊的名医，我对仙舟各地有什么好吃好玩的地方真是了如指掌，雇了我可绝不会让你吃亏的！
t_en: Mm-hm, as a renowned doctor, I regularly go out for street food. A—After I'm done treating my patients, that is! Anyway, basically I'm an expert on all the culinary delights the Xianzhou has to offer, so... Ugh, hire me as your tour guide already!

t_ch: 你不是…医生吗？
t_en: Aren't you... a doctor?

t_ch: 有什么关系嘛！外出看诊的名医，也会对仙舟各地有什么好吃好玩的地方了如指掌啊。雇了我可绝不会让你吃亏的！
t_en: Yeah, so what? I go out all the time to see patients, so I know the food establishments around here like the back of my hand. I'm the best tour guide around!

t_ch: 写作导游，读作吃白食的。
t_en: "Tour guide"? Sounds like a fancy way of getting free food.

t_ch: 竟、竟然一眼看穿了我的想法……
t_en: ...Eh-he, was it that obvious...?

t_ch: 但多一个人又有什么关系嘛，作为到处吃喝…咳，外出看诊的名医，我对仙舟各地有什么好吃好玩的地方了如指掌，雇了我可绝不会让你吃亏的！
t_en: But the more the merrier, right? I go out all the time to see patients, so I know the food establishments around here like the back of my hand. I'm the best tour guide you could hope for!

t_ch: 一路上我分文不取，当然当然，如果你要吃点什么记得带上我就好~
t_en: I won't charge anything for my services... except for food, of course!

t_ch: 如果在游玩途中遇到些肠胃不适、跌打损伤的小问题，也有我这样超专业的医士为您立刻诊治，这样的好事哪里能找到喔！
t_en: And if you get into medical trouble — gastrointestinal discomfort, sprained ankle, yadi yada — you'll have a professional doctor right by your side! That's a two-in-one deal!

t_ch: 金人巷？什么金人巷？
t_en: Aurum Alley? What's that?

t_ch: 什么嘛，你到了长乐天都还没有去过金人巷吗？
t_en: Wait, you've been to Exalting Sanctum before but you never visited Aurum Alley?

t_ch: 我忙得很，没有闲逛的功夫。
t_en: I'm too busy to wander around random places.

t_ch: 咦，真的吗？你都来到了长乐天，却没空闲逛，真是可怜……
t_en: Really? You didn't have time to look around? What a pity...

t_ch: 我以为长乐天就这么点儿地方。
t_en: I didn't know there was anything else to see in Exalting Sanctum.

t_ch: 当然不是，怎么可能嘛。
t_en: Didn't know? There's lots to see around here!

t_ch: 看来像你这样刚刚抵达仙舟的旅客一定需要一个导游！
t_en: Looks like you're in need of a professional tour guide!

t_ch: 你不是…医生吗？
t_en: Aren't you... a doctor?

t_ch: 有什么关系嘛！外出看诊的名医，也会对仙舟各地有什么好吃好玩的地方真是了如指掌啊。雇了我可绝不会让你吃亏的！
t_en: Yeah, so what? I go out all the time to see patients, so I know the food establishments around here like the back of my hand. I'm the best tour guide around!

t_ch: 写作导游，读作吃白食的。
t_en: "Tour guide"? Sounds like a fancy way of getting free food.

t_ch: 竟、竟然一眼看穿了我的想法……
t_en: ...Eh-he, was it that obvious...?

t_ch: 但多一个人又有什么关系嘛，作为到处吃喝…咳，外出看诊的名医，我对仙舟各地有什么好吃好玩的地方了如指掌，雇了我可绝不会让你吃亏的！
t_en: But the more the merrier, right? I go out all the time to see patients, so I know the food establishments around here like the back of my hand. I'm the best tour guide you could hope for!

t_ch: 一路上我分文不取，当然当然，如果你要吃点什么记得带上我就好~
t_en: I won't charge anything for my services... except for food, of course!

t_ch: 如果在游玩途中遇到些肠胃不适、跌打损伤的小问题，也有我这样超专业的医士为您立刻诊治，这样的好事哪里能找到喔！
t_en: And if you get into medical trouble — gastrointestinal discomfort, sprained ankle, yadi yada — you'll have a professional doctor right by your side! That's a two-in-one deal!

t_ch: 首先声明，我不请客。
t_en: Before you continue — I'm not a free meal ticket.

t_ch: 竟、竟然一眼看穿了我的想法……
t_en: ...Eh-he, was it that obvious...?

t_ch: 但多一个人又有什么关系嘛，作为到处吃喝…咳，外出看诊的名医，我对仙舟各地有什么好吃好玩的地方了如指掌，让我当你的导游可绝不会吃亏的！
t_en: But the more the merrier, right? I go out all the time to see patients, so I know the food establishments around here like the back of my hand. I'm the best tour guide you could hope for!

t_ch: 如果在游玩途中遇到些肠胃不适、跌打损伤的小问题，也有我这样超专业的医士为您立刻诊治，这样的好事哪里能找到喔！
t_en: And if you get into medical trouble — gastrointestinal discomfort, sprained ankle, yadi yada — you'll have a professional doctor right by your side! That's a two-in-one deal!

t_ch: 什么一站式医生导游…
t_en: A two-in-one tour guide doctor?

t_ch: 这叫「关怀备至」。
t_en: It's called an "all-inclusive package."

t_ch: 看来不带上你是不行了…
t_en: Sounds like an offer I can't refuse...

t_ch: 好耶！
t_en: Yay!

t_ch: 那咱们这就走吧？
t_en: Let the food tour begin.

t_ch: 好耶！
t_en: Yay!

t_ch: 快点快点，要是去晚了小吃都卖完了怎么办？
t_en: Hurry up! Do you want the snacks to sell out!?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}游客，咱们到啦，这儿就是金人巷了！要论旅游项目的特色，当然就是「好吃的特别多」。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, we've reached our destination — Aurum Alley! Of course, the main feature of this tour is the endless selection of tasty delicacies.

t_ch: 尤其是「尚滋味」的江湖菜，这样的味道整个罗浮仙舟也只有金人巷这里才能吃得到。
t_en: The folk dishes at Spices Supreme are a must-try. This is the only place on the Luofu you can find them.

t_ch: 所以，话既然说到了这里……
t_en: So, now that we're here...

t_ch: 你这么会吃，推荐推荐？
t_en: You're the tour guide. What do you recommend?

t_ch: …推荐推荐？你应该说「我请你吃点好吃的」。
t_en: ...Recommend? I think you mean: "I'd like to treat you to some Xianzhou delicacies."

t_ch: 你想让我请你吃什么？
t_en: *sigh* Which Xianzhou delicacies would you like?

t_ch: 蒜苗五花肉脆瓜胡辣鸡丁星芋扣肉红油乱斩牛杂陈婆豆腐…哎，我舌头都要打结了！背药名都没这么利索的……
t_en: Ooh! Garlic-infused Pork, Crisped Melon and Diced Chicken, Star Taro Braised Pork, Chili Oil Beef Offal Stew, Granny Chen's Tofu... Ugh, I'm getting tired saying them all! Reciting medicine ingredients is easier than this!

t_ch: 当然了，我们可以先去小吃摊上垫个底，你可以先想想自个有什么想吃的，我找个空位子等你。
t_en: Let's start with the snack stands. You can browse around for bit — I'll go find us a seat while you make up your mind.

t_ch: （总之，先向食客们打听打听吧……）
t_en: (I should see what the other customers have to say about the food here...)

t_ch: 这旅游攻略好像还没有更新啊。
t_en: This travel guide needs an update.

t_ch: 不好意思，打听一下…
t_en: Excuse me...

t_ch: …你问我小吃摊上有什么东西好吃吗？
t_en: ...Oh, you wanna know which snack stalls I recommend around here?

t_ch: 这你可问对人了，虽然我跟你一样都是初来乍到，但是这里的美食我都尝了一遍。
t_en: Well, you've come to the right person. It's my first time here too, but I've tried everything.

t_ch: 金人巷有很多食物我从来都没有听过，第一次吃的时候，其实还有些害怕。
t_en: There's a lot of things here that I've never even heard of — I was kinda scared to try them.

t_ch: 有什么好吃的吗？
t_en: Is there anything good?

t_ch: 你可以试试「貘馍卷」，看上去就像是蛋糕卷一样。小吃摊老板说这是用「梦貘」做的，那到底是什么生物呀？
t_en: I recommend the tuskpir wraps — they're very similar to cake rolls, but the hawker said they're made from something called... well, "tuskpir..." What do you suppose that is?

t_ch: 当时我整个人都懵了。我犹豫再三，一咬牙、一跺脚、狠狠心吃下去之后，发现这「貘馍卷」就是谷物小点心，什么嘛，差点被老板骗了。
t_en: I'll admit, I was hesitant to try it at first. But once I worked up the courage and took a bite, it turned out to be just another delicious snack.

t_ch: 不过味道倒是意外独特，咬口酥脆，入口即化。吃下肚片刻还会对过去品尝美食的回忆产生一些联想。所以说，这真的是「梦貘」做的吗……
t_en: Very unique taste — crispy melt-in-the-mouth type texture too... Y'know, the flavor kinda reminded me of a lot of stuff I'd eaten before, but I couldn't put my finger on anything. That's the magic of "tuskpir," I guess...

t_ch: 有踩到什么雷吗？
t_en: Is there anything I should avoid?

t_ch: 你听说过「苏打豆汁儿」吗？仙舟本地人总说这玩意儿可好喝了。我买来喝了一口，喷了邻桌食客一身。
t_en: Have you heard of mung bean soda? The Xianzhou locals say it's delicious. So I bought one, took a sip, and then sprayed it all over the diners at the next table.

t_ch: 「苏打豆汁儿」颜色灰绿，味道酸腐，我看这东西只有一些本地老饕能受得了，您呀，也别抱着兴趣随便尝试了。
t_en: It has a grayish green color and a sour, rotten flavor... I reckon there's a handful of food connoisseurs that actually like it. Try some if you dare, but don't get your hopes up.

t_ch: 味道还是一点没变。
t_en: *sigh* Same great taste...

t_ch: 不好意思，打听一下…
t_en: Excuse me...

t_ch: …你问有什么好吃的吗？金人巷这地方，你闭着眼睛随便走，哪一家都好吃。
t_en: ...Oh, interested in the food? In Aurum Alley, there's no such thing as a bad food stall. Just close your eyes and pick one at random.

t_ch: 我叫霄翰，虽说是金人巷本地人，但也好久没有回来了。离家在外的时候，光是想着这里的味道，乡愁便让我彻夜难眠。
t_en: My name's Xiaohan. I'm originally from Aurum Alley, but this is my first visit in a long time. When I was away, I'd stay up at night reminiscing about the flavors and aromas of this place.

t_ch: 有什么好吃的推荐吗？
t_en: Do you have any recommendations?

t_ch: 你是温和派、还是激进派？
t_en: Are you feeling adventurous or cautious?

t_ch: 温和的就行。
t_en: A little caution can't hurt.

t_ch: 那我推荐你去点一碗「热浮羊奶」，甜甜的，不腻口。喝下肚去还会觉得自己体重变轻了——当然，不能替代减肥餐。
t_en: Then I recommend trying a bowl of steamed puffergoat milk. It has a mild, sweet flavor and a smooth texture. People say they feel lighter after drinking it! Of course, it's no substitute for a balanced diet.

t_ch: 激进的，有多激进？
t_en: Depends on what you mean by "adventurous."

t_ch: 你要是想要激进的，就得尝尝「苏打豆汁儿」了。
t_en: If you're really feeling adventurous, you could try some mung bean soda.

t_ch: 这豆汁儿啊，要尝它的「酸」，酸中带…怪的味道。
t_en: I'd describe the taste as... unique. And a little sour.

t_ch: 你还可以试着大口猛灌…任由这饮料在肚子里翻江倒海。那滋味，妙极了。
t_en: It's best to drink it in big gulps, and let it fizzle in your stomach. That's a great feeling.

t_ch: 没想到，您这是游子归乡。
t_en: So you're a wanderer returning home.

t_ch: 仙舟上有化外民来观光，我们也得去其他世界看看，多听多看多交流嘛。
t_en: The Xianzhou gets plenty of visitors, so it's only natural that we should venture out and see what other worlds are like.

t_ch: 可惜离家百年归来，只觉得这金人巷越发萧瑟陌生。只有「苏打豆汁儿」还算是一如既往。
t_en: I spent <unbreak>100</unbreak> years away from home, and I have to say... Aurum Alley has seen better days. It's looking kind of run-down. The only thing that hasn't changed is the mung bean soda.

t_ch: 我才第一次来，不觉得它冷清。
t_en: I don't think it's that bad.

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈，这不过是我的归乡感受罢了。
t_en: Haha, maybe I'm just nostalgic.

t_ch: 以前这里很繁华吗？
t_en: Was this place more prosperous back then?

t_ch: 没错，许久以前，这金人巷曾是罗浮第一夜市。
t_en: That it was. Many years ago, Aurum Alley was the most popular night market on the Luofu.

t_ch: 虽说我在外面事业小成，但我始终爱着这金人巷，终究还是回来了。
t_en: I had a good career going out on the other worlds, but I always missed the Alley. In the end, I decided to come back.

t_ch: …我想着能不能出一份力，让这里变得更美好一些。
t_en: ...Now I'm keen to do my part and make this place a little better.

t_ch: 啧啧…啧啧啧……
t_en: Tsk...

t_ch: 这店家到底懂不懂什么是流量，懂不懂什么是经济啊！
t_en: Do these people know nothing about the value of brand exposure? That's marketing <unbreak>101</unbreak>!

t_ch: 不好意思，打听一下…
t_en: I'm wondering what's good to eat around here...

t_ch: 我是不是在哪里见过你啊？
t_en: Have I seen you somewhere before?

t_ch: 见过我？不不不，你没见过我…哎呀，你在这杵着就是为了想打听我的建议，对吧？
t_en: Me? No, no, no, no, no, we've never met... Oh, are you wondering what's good to eat around here?

t_ch: 你问我小吃，算是问对人了，我是大名鼎鼎的「食探」，最擅长去大小饭馆里探店。
t_en: Well, I can help you with that — I'm known around here as the "forager" — I venture into all manner of culinary establishments to taste test their food.

t_ch: 可惜这家老板非要说我吃白食，不让我探店，活该后悔去吧。
t_en: The seller at this food stall won't let me do any taste testing because I'm not a "paying customer." Well, if they don't want free publicity, that's their loss.

t_ch: 有什么好吃的推荐吗？
t_en: Do you have any recommendations?

t_ch: 那你就信我的，就点「琼实鸟串」或者「鸣藕糕」，绝对不会出错。
t_en: Ah, with my discriminating taste as your guide, you'll never go wrong. Give the berrypheasant skewers or songlotus cake a try.

t_ch: 探店不是自己花钱吗……
t_en: Don't taste testers pay for their own food?

t_ch: 跟你话说不到一块去。我探店，其实是在帮店家做宣传，这本来是要收钱的。
t_en: You're clearly not familiar with this line of work. Taste testing gives the restaurant publicity — that means a higher sales conversion!

t_ch: …算了算了，我也不计较了，我到别处探店去。
t_en: ...Ah, forget it. There are other food vendors here waiting for me to appraise them...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，买小吃的事就拜托你了，别让我等太久啦。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, I'm counting on you to go buy all the snacks — don't keep me waiting too long!

t_ch: 哎哟，这位客官是化外民游客吧。我想问您一句，喜欢金人巷这地方吗？
t_en: Oh, a visitor from afar? May I ask you a question — do you like Aurum Alley?

t_ch: 才刚来，还谈不上喜欢。
t_en: It's my first visit here, I can't say yet.

t_ch: 那我可得好好招待您。
t_en: Then I'll have to make sure your first visit here is a good one.

t_ch: 喜欢，这里的人都很友好。
t_en: Yes, the people here are very friendly.

t_ch: 那就好，那就好。我们对远道而来的客人都是很热情的。
t_en: Glad to hear. We always try to treat visitors from outside well.

t_ch: …说实话，不太喜欢这里。
t_en: ...To be honest, not really.

t_ch: 那还真是挺可惜的。
t_en: Oh, well, that's regrettable.

t_ch: …客人别嫌我唠叨，你从外面的世界来的，了解星际和平公司吗，风评好吗？
t_en: ...I hope you don't mind me prattling on, but since you're an Outworlder, are you familiar with the IPC? Do you know if it has a good reputation?

t_ch: 公司对银河贸易帮助巨大。
t_en: It's been a great help to pan-cosmic business.

t_ch: 确实，巡镝和信用点之间兑换也很方便。
t_en: That's true, and they made it much more convenient to exchange strale and credits.

t_ch: 资本巨鳄，感觉不是好东西。
t_en: They're a cabal of predatory sharks.

t_ch: 我听会长说「公司会毁掉金人巷」，我有些担心。
t_en: I was a little worried when I heard the president say that the IPC is going to destroy Aurum Alley.

t_ch: …只记得查宝的广告非常烦人。
t_en: ...All I know is its Findie ads are really annoying.

t_ch: 查宝？我不知道那是什么。
t_en: Findie? I've never heard of that before.

t_ch: 换句话说，你其实也不太了解星际和平公司……
t_en: Anyway, sounds like you know as much about them as I do...

t_ch: 哎呀，我也不能把你晾在这里…你看看菜单，选选想吃什么吧，算是阿姨送你的。
t_en: Well, I can't just leave you here empty handed — take a look at the menu. Order whatever you want — it's on the house.

t_ch: 来一份「貘馍卷」。
t_en: I'll have a tuskpir wrap.

t_ch: 来一份「鸣藕糕」。
t_en: I'll have a songlotus cake.

t_ch: 来一份「琼实鸟串」。
t_en: I'll have a berrypheasant skewer.

t_ch: 来一份「热浮羊奶」。
t_en: I'll have some steamed puffergoat milk.

t_ch: 来一份「苏打豆汁儿」。
t_en: I'll have some mung bean soda.

t_ch: 好嘞。
t_en: Alrighty.

t_ch: 有什么好吃的吗？
t_en: So, is there anything good?

t_ch: （取出小吃）
t_en: (Take out the snack)

t_ch: 啊，太好了，你怎么知道我想吃什么？
t_en: Fantastic! Hey, how did you know my favorite food?

t_ch: 我最懂你啦。
t_en: I just know you that well.

t_ch: 只是刚好而已。
t_en: Lucky guess.

t_ch: 我觉得你是什么都想吃。
t_en: I'm pretty sure you'll eat anything.

t_ch: 太好了…但你好像只买了一份，怎么办？
t_en: Great... but it looks like you only brought enough for one person... what am I supposed to do?

t_ch: 你吃吧，我不饿。
t_en: You can have it — I'm not really hungry.

t_ch: 这多不好意思啊。
t_en: Alright, if you say so!
